jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - API Experience
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco, San Jose, or Bellevue office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.


What The Role is About

The API Experience team is responsible for Lambda’s customer-facing Core Cloud APIs — everything from creating a new instance or attaching storage to managing VPCs. We deliver a delightful, consistent, and cohesive API-first experience to all our customers, from self-serve Public Cloud users to the largest, most demanding AI companies on the planet.

What You’ll Do

  • Build end-to-end features for Lambda Cloud.

  • Contribute to both frontend and backend code across existing and new features.

  • Collaborate with Product Engineering, Platform, and Infrastructure teams to deliver solutions that cut across the stack.

  • Partner with Product Managers to design, scope, and deliver features that are critical to customer success, without compromising on our product quality.

  • Work directly with Superintelligence customers’ engineering teams.

  • Own production features and systems, including participating in incident response rotations.

  • Design and implement observability and maintenance processes for the new features we build.

You

  • Backend experience building business-critical services using Python, Unix/Command line, Django or FastAPI, and Postgres.

  • Proven experience owning complex features end-to-end, from defining problems with Product to GA release and measuring success criteria, in highly ambiguous environments under tight deadlines.

  • Proven ability to clearly communicate trade offs in scoping and timelines.

  • Experience designing, implementing, and maintaining developer-facing APIs and documentation.

Additional Core Experience

  • CI/CD: Automation for testing and deployment workflows

  • Reliability & Observability: Logging, alerting, and building highly available systems

  • Cloud Native Services: Experience with AWS, Okta, Cloudflare, etc.

Nice-to-Haves

  • Experience with Kubernetes

  • Infrastructure as Code (e.g.,Terraform, Atlantis, Crossplane)

  • Experience in ML, AI, or data science

  • Application security and web hardening

  • Engineering leadership or mentorship experience

jobs.title: Head of Corporate Strategy
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco or San Jose office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.

The Opportunity

We're looking for a Head of Corporate Strategy to serve as a key strategic thought partner to the SVP of Commercial Strategy and Lambda's senior leadership team. As we scale rapidly across GPU cloud, data center infrastructure, and AI systems, we need a seasoned strategist who can independently own Lambda's corporate strategy function—synthesizing market intelligence, competitive dynamics, and internal data into clear strategic choices—and drive high-priority cross-functional initiatives from concept to execution. This role sits at the intersection of long-range planning, commercial strategy, M&A and technology evaluation, executive communication, and will have high visibility with the CEO and C-suite.

What You'll Do

  • Lead Corporate Strategy & Long-Range Planning

    • Own Lambda's annual and multi-year strategic planning process, synthesizing input from commercial, product, finance, and operations into a cohesive corporate strategy

    • Identify and size emerging opportunities in AI infrastructure, GPU cloud, and adjacent markets; develop strategic frameworks to guide investment and build/buy/partner decisions

    • Lead competitive intelligence efforts—tracking hyperscalers, co-location providers, GPU cloud competitors, and AI-native challengers—and translate insights into executive recommendations

    • Partner with the CFO and Finance to align the long-range strategic plan with the financial model and capital allocation priorities

  • Drive Executive Communication & Stakeholder Alignment

    • Develop board-level strategy materials and CEO decision-support documents

    • Drive alignment across senior leadership on strategic priorities; create clear frameworks to resolve competing resource and investment decisions

    • Prepare the CEO and SVP for external strategy conversations, including customer EBRs, partner discussions, and industry forums

    • Support Commercial Strategy

    • Partner with the SVP of Commercial Strategy on go-to-market architecture, pricing strategy, and large deal structuring for enterprise and hyperscaler accounts

    • Lead cross-functional projects on behalf of the SVP, ensuring workstreams deliver on time and at C-suite quality bar

  • Support Strategic Technology Evaluation

    • Own the framework for evaluating long range strategic technology areas that maybe synergistic with Lambda’s current business —from initial screening and thesis development through diligence and recommendation. This includes assess emerging technologies, AI software platforms, hardware capabilities, and infrastructure tooling for strategic fit, competitive differentiation, and build/buy/partner trade-offs

    • Develop and maintain a structured pipeline of potential partnership opportunities in AI infrastructure, silicon, networking, and adjacent software markets while working closely with the corporate development team on potential M&A fit.

    • Lead financial and strategic diligence workstreams: market sizing, competitive positioning, synergy modeling, integration risk assessment, and long-range value creation scenarios

    • Build Market & Organizational Intelligence

    • Build and maintain Lambda's strategic market model—tracking TAM/SAM, competitive positioning, win/loss patterns, and customer segment dynamics

    • Synthesize signals from Sales, Product, Data Center Ops, and Finance into strategic insight for senior leadership

    • Develop Lambda's perspective on macro trends shaping AI infrastructure demand: model training workloads, inference at scale, sovereign AI, and enterprise adoption curves

    • Mentor and develop strategy analysts and associates; scale team output as the function grows

What You'll Have

  • 10+ years of experience across strategy consulting (McKinsey, BCG, Bain, or equivalent), investment banking, and/or corporate strategy at a high-growth or publicly traded technology company

  • Deep expertise in AI, cloud infrastructure, or data center markets; ability to independently develop and own market models, competitive landscapes, and investment theses

  • Demonstrated track record of independently translating ambiguous, high-stakes strategic questions into boardroom-ready frameworks and executive recommendations

  • Experience leading large, cross-functional strategic initiatives with direct C-suite accountability and material impact on company direction

  • Familiarity evaluating AI software, hardware, or infrastructure technologies for strategic fit; ability to engage credibly with technical teams on architecture and capability assessments

  • Prior role as a right-hand strategic partner to a CCO, CRO, or President in a commercial strategy capacity is valued

  • Track record of building, mentoring, or managing strategy teams; ability to develop junior talent and scale team output

  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; proven ability to develop and deliver board-level strategy materials

  • Operates with VP-adjacent ownership—sets the agenda, manages ambiguity, and builds the function rather than just executing within it

Education & Credentials

  • Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Business, or related field required

  • MBA from a top-tier institution strongly preferred

  • Prior experience in a structured analytical training program (consulting, banking, or equivalent) strongly preferred

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • We offer generous cash & equity compensation

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Lambda is an Equal Opportunity employer. Applicants are considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, age, sex, gender, marital status, sexual orientation and identity, genetic information, veteran status, citizenship, or any other factors prohibited by local, state, or federal law.

jobs.title: Senior Human Resources Generalist
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.

About the Role

The HR Operations team is engaged in the administration of a broad range of HR activities associated with the employee lifecycle. The team acts as the first point of contact for queries from managers and staff across the company and also provides a comprehensive administrative support service to the HR team, supporting all projects undertaken with the HR Department.

As a Senior HR Generalist at Lambda, you will play a key role in delivering a high-quality and responsive HR service, ensuring HRIS data integrity, automating workflows, and continuously improving HR processes to support scalability, sustainability, and simplicity.

What You’ll Do

  • Champion and actively reinforce Lambda’s culture, mission, purpose, and values across the organization, serving as a trusted partner to leaders and employees

  • Establish, evolve, and enforce company policies, ensuring alignment with business needs, compliance requirements, and best practices

  • Support the implementation, optimization, and ongoing management of HR systems, including Workday (HRIS) and ADP (payroll), ensuring data accuracy, process efficiency, and strong user experience

  • Ensure the integrity and maintenance of employee data across HR systems (e.g., HRIS, shared drives, knowledge management tools), with a focus on accuracy, security, and scalability.

  • Serve as a point of contact for employees on HR, benefits, and policy-related matters, providing guidance and driving resolution through HR shared channels and ticketing systems

  • Support compliance efforts for annual filings, reporting, and employee communications, partnering cross-functionally as needed

  • Act as the HR Operations point of contact for internal and external audits (e.g., SOC 2 Type I & II, ISO 27001), including maintaining documentation, responding to audit requests, and strengthening controls and processes

  • Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned, operating with a high degree of ownership and autonomy

You

  • Have a Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience (1 year of work experience for every school year)

  • Have 5+ years of experience in HR Operations roles

  • Fluency in Workday, ADP, JIRA and Google Workspace

  • Have excellent communication, time management, and organizational skills

  • Can maintain high degrees of integrity handling sensitive and confidential information daily

  • Have proficiency in establishing, implementing, and enforcing company policies

  • Have the ability to collaborate, navigate ambiguity, and adapt to change, and balance

  • multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

  • Have strong relationship building and networking skills

  • Can manage ambiguity across multiple priorities and to know when to escalate issues for final resolution

  • Experience in the technology industry

  • Start-up and / or remote environment experience

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Lambda is an Equal Opportunity employer. jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - IAM
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.


*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.


Role Summary

Build and scale Lambda’s Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform, enabling secure, reliable and intuitive access control across all products and customers.

As a Senior Engineer, you will own the design and implementation of core IAM capabilities such as RBAC, authentication flows, and identity lifecycle systems. You’ll work within Lambda’s broader IAM architecture to deliver systems that are secure, scalable and easy for developers and customers to use.

This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy working on security-critical systems, care deeply about correctness and usability, and can independently drive complex features from design through production. IAM is a critical foundation for Lambda’s enterprise readiness and will shape how customers securely access compute, data, and infrastructure at scale.

What you’ll Do

  • Design and implement core IAM components including identity models, authentication flows and authorization systems

  • Build and evolve enterprise IAM features such as RBAC, SSO (SAML/OIDC), SCIM provisioning, MFA, and audit logging

  • Own end-to-end delivery of IAM features from technical design to production rollout and monitoring

  • Collaborate with teams across API Experience, Billing, Core Cloud Platform, Console Experience, Infrastructure and Security to integrate consistent identity and access patterns across systems

  • Contribute to API design and ensure consistency in identity and permission semantics across services

  • Participate in design reviews and provide thoughtful input on system architecture and tradeoffs

  • Debug and resolve complex production issues in security-sensitive systems

  • Improve system reliability, observability, and operational workflows for IAM services

  • Mentor junior engineers and contribute to raising the bar on engineering quality

You (Must-Haves)

  • 5+ years of backend or platform engineering experience

  • Strong experience building or working on authentication and/or authorization systems

  • Solid understanding of IAM fundamentals:

    • Authentication (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML)

    • Authorization (RBAC, ABAC, or similar models)

    • Identity lifecycle concepts (provisioning, deprovisioning)

  • Experience building distributed systems in a cloud or SaaS environment

  • Strong coding skills in Python or Go, with experience building production backend systems

  • Experience designing APIs and working with service-oriented architectures

  • Ability to independently own projects and deliver high-quality systems

  • Strong debugging, problem-solving, and communication skills

Nice-to-Haves

  • Experience with enterprise IAM features (SSO, SCIM, MFA, audit logs)

  • Familiarity with identity providers (Okta, Auth0, WorkOS)

  • Exposure to multi-tenant system design

  • Experience working on developer platforms or infrastructure products

  • Understanding of security best practices in cloud environments

  • Ability to interact directly with customers and partner with product to understand their IAM requirements

  • Strong academic background (EECS, Math, Software Engineering, Physics, or related fields)

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • We offer generous cash & equity compensation

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

jobs.title: Controller
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in both our San Francisco and San Jose office locations, for a combined 4 days per week.

Lambda is seeking an experienced Controller to lead and scale our accounting organization during a pivotal stage of growth. Reporting to the Chief Accounting Officer, this role will play a critical leadership position in building a best-in-class controllership function to support a rapidly expanding, multi-billion-dollar AI infrastructure platform.

As Lambda continues to scale GPU cloud services, enterprise contracts, and capital-intensive data center infrastructure, this role will ensure accounting excellence and operational rigor built to support long-term strategic ambitions. The Controller will bring strong judgment, deep operational expertise, and the ability to build systems, processes, and teams that operate at public-company standards.

This is a hands-on leadership opportunity for someone excited to build, elevate, and modernize a high-performing accounting organization in a dynamic, fast-scaling environment.

What You’ll Do

  • Lead all aspects of accounting operations, including general ledger, revenue recognition, fixed assets, and lease accounting.

  • Oversee a timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual close process across multiple entities and product lines

  • Lead the evolution of systems, automation, AI, and ERP optimization to improve accounting’s accuracy, efficiency, and scalability

  • Design, implement, and continuously strengthen internal controls and accounting policies to support SOX compliance and long-term reporting scalability

  • Partner closely with Legal, FP&A, Operations, Supply Chain, Treasury, and other cross-functional leaders to ensure accounting implications are thoughtfully considered in strategic decisions

  • Establish and manage relationships with external auditors and help oversee annual audits and quarterly reviews

  • Build, mentor, and develop a high-performing accounting team with a culture of accountability, operational excellence, and continuous improvement

  • Serve as a strategic thought partner to the Chief Accounting Officer and executive leadership on accounting matters

  • Also serve as a partner to the Head of Technical Accounting and Financial Reporting, and the Head of Tax, both of whom also report to the Chief Accounting Officer.

You

  • 15+ years of progressive accounting experience, including public accounting and senior leadership in a high-growth technology company

  • Experience operating in or preparing an organization for public-company standards

  • Deep technical expertise in US GAAP, including revenue recognition, fixed assets, leases, and complex commercial arrangements

  • Strong knowledge of internal controls and SOX compliance

  • Proven ability to build and scale accounting teams in fast-moving environments

  • CPA required; Big 4 background strongly preferred

  • Strong executive presence and ability to communicate complex accounting topics clearly to cross-functional leaders

Nice to Have

  • Experience in AI infrastructure, cloud computing, hardware, or other capital-intensive environments

  • Experience with multi-entity and international operations

  • ERP implementation or post-implementation optimization experience (NetSuite preferred)

  • Experience partnering closely with Treasury and Capital Markets functions

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • We offer generous cash & equity compensation

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

jobs.title: Accounting Manager - Fixed Assets and Leases
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires flexible presence in both our San Francisco and San Jose offices, a total of 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.

We are seeking an experienced Fixed Asset & Lease Accounting Manager to lead the accounting, controls, and reporting for our capital asset and lease portfolios in a highly capital-intensive infrastructure environment.

The company maintains over $1.5B in fixed assets placed into service, more than $200M in Construction in Progress (CIP), and $500M+ in operating Right-of-Use (ROU) assets related to data center leases across 15+ locations in the U.S. and internationally. The majority of our fixed assets consist of GPU compute infrastructure, along with associated racks, cabling, and supporting data center equipment.

This role will own the full lifecycle of fixed assets and leases—from capitalization policy and CIP monitoring to depreciation, lease accounting under ASC 842, asset disposals, and audit support. The ideal candidate brings strong technical accounting expertise, operational rigor, and the ability to partner cross-functionally with infrastructure, procurement, and legal teams in a rapidly scaling environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Fixed Asset Accounting & Controls

    • Manage accounting and reporting for $1.5B+ in fixed assets including GPU infrastructure, racks, cabling, and related equipment.

    • Oversee asset capitalization, classification, and useful life determinations in accordance with U.S. GAAP.

    • Review and approve asset additions, transfers, disposals, and retirements.

    • Ensure accurate depreciation calculations and reconciliation of the fixed asset subledger to the general ledger.

    • Maintain and continuously improve capitalization policies and internal controls.

  • Construction in Progress (CIP) Management

    • Oversee accounting for $200M+ in CIP related to GPU infrastructure deployments and data center buildouts.

    • Track asset commissioning and ensure timely capitalization once assets are placed into service.

    • Partner with engineering, infrastructure, and procurement teams to validate deployment milestones and cost allocations.

    • Monitor CIP aging and ensure appropriate classification and capitalization timing.

  • Lease Accounting (ASC 842)

    • Manage accounting and reporting for $500M+ in Right-of-Use (ROU) assets related to data center leases across 15+ locations.

    • Ensure compliance with ASC 842 including lease classification, ROU asset and liability calculations, and ongoing lease reassessments.

    • Abstract lease agreements and identify embedded leases where applicable.

    • Coordinate with technical accounting for lease modifications, renewals, and terminations.

    • Maintain lease accounting schedules and support financial reporting disclosures.

  • Financial Reporting & Audit Support

    • Prepare fixed asset and lease accounting schedules for monthly, quarterly, and annual close.

    • Support internal and external audit processes by providing documentation, reconciliations, and analysis.

    • Assist with financial statement disclosures related to fixed assets, depreciation, and leases.

    • Partner with tax and finance teams on asset-related reporting requirements.

  • Asset Lifecycle & Operational Partnership

    • Collaborate with infrastructure, operations, and procurement teams to ensure accurate tracking of high-value GPU assets.

    • Support processes for asset tagging, inventory tracking, and periodic physical verification of assets.

    • Oversee asset retirement and decommissioning processes for technology equipment.

  • Process Improvement & Systems

    • Evaluate and enhance fixed asset and lease accounting systems and workflows to support rapid growth.

    • Drive automation and integration between ERP (NetSuite), procurement systems, and asset tracking tools.

    • Develop scalable processes to manage high volumes of infrastructure assets

Qualifications

  • Required

    • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance.

    • 7+ years of accounting experience with significant exposure to fixed asset and lease accounting.

    • Experience managing large-scale fixed asset and lease portfolios ($2B+ preferred).

    • Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP fixed asset accounting and ASC 842 lease accounting.

    • Experience managing Construction in Progress (CIP) and capital project accounting.

    • Proficiency with ERP systems and fixed asset management modules.

    • Strong analytical, organizational, and process improvement skills.

  • Preferred

    • CPA with public accounting experience.

    • Experience in data center, cloud infrastructure, technology hardware, telecom, or other capital-intensive industries.

    • Experience accounting for technology infrastructure assets such as servers, GPUs, or networking equipment.

    • Experience with global operations and multi-location lease portfolios.

    • Experience implementing or optimizing fixed asset or lease accounting systems.

    • Forward-thinker in embracing and implementing AI tools as part of foundational accounting processes.

Key Competencies

  • Technical accounting expertise (fixed assets and leases)

  • Strong internal controls mindset

  • Ability to manage complex, high-value asset portfolios

  • Cross-functional collaboration with operational teams

  • Detail-oriented with strong analytical capability

  • Ability to operate effectively in a fast-growing environment

  • Forward-thinker in embracing and implementing AI tools as part of foundation accounting processes.

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • We offer generous cash & equity compensation

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Lambda is an Equal Opportunity employer. Applicants are considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, age, sex, gender, marital status, sexual orientation and identity, genetic information, veteran status, citizenship, or any other factors prohibited by local, state, or federal law.

jobs.title: Data Center Operations Systems Engineer (Quebec City, Canada)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our Quebec City, Canada Data Center 5 days; shift work

What You'll Do

  • Ensure new server, storage and network infrastructure is properly racked, labeled, cabled, and configured

  • Troubleshoot hardware and software issues in some of the world’s most advanced systems

  • Document data center layout and network topology in DCIM software

  • Work with supply chain & manufacturing teams to ensure timely deployment of systems and project plans for large-scale deployments

  • Manage a parts depot inventory and track equipment through the delivery-store-stage-deploy-handoff process in each of our data centers

  • Work closely with HW Support team to ensure data center infrastructure-related support tickets are resolved

  • Work with RMA team to ensure faulty parts are returned and replacements are ordered

  • Follow installation standards and documentation for placement, labeling, and cabling to drive consistency and discoverability across all data centers

You

  • Are familiar with critical infrastructure systems supporting data centers, such as power distribution, air flow management, environmental monitoring, capacity planning, DCIM software, structured cabling, and cable management

  • Are someone who pays attention to detail and has the ability to follow instructions

  • Are action-oriented and have a strong willingness to learn

  • Are willing to travel for bring up of new data center locations

Nice to Have

  • Experience with troubleshooting server hardware

  • Experience with/or knowledge of network topology

  • Familiarity with ticketing systems like JIRA and Zendesk

  • Experience with Linux administration

  • Experience with working in large-scale distributed data center environments

  • Experience with Supermicro & Nvidia hardware

jobs.title: Senior Data Center Operations Systems Engineer (Quebec City, Canada)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our Quebec City, Canada Data Center 5 days per week; shift work.

What You'll Do

  • Ensure new server, storage and network infrastructure is properly racked, labeled, cabled, and configured.

  • Troubleshoot hardware and software issues in some of the world’s most advanced GPU and Networking systems.

  • Document and update data center layout and network topology in DCIM software

  • Work with supply chain & manufacturing teams to ensure timely deployment of systems and project plans for large-scale deployments

  • Manage a parts depot inventory and track equipment through the delivery-store-stage-deploy-handoff process in each of our data centers

  • Partner with HW Support teams to ensure data center hardware incidents with higher level troubleshooting challenges are resolved, reported on and solutions are disseminated to the large operations organization.

  • Work with RMA team to ensure faulty parts are returned and replacements are ordered

  • Follow installation standards and documentation for placement, labeling, and cabling to drive consistency and discoverability across all data centers

You

  • Have strong past experiences with critical infrastructure systems supporting data centers, such as power distribution, air flow management, environmental monitoring, capacity planning, DCIM software, structured cabling, and cable management

  • Be familiar with carrier DIA circuit test and turn ups, fiber testing and troubleshooting

  • Basic knowledge of cable optics and the different types of use

  • Solid understanding of single and three phase power theories

  • PDU balancing and why it is important

  • Familiar with multiple cable media types and their uses

  • Knowledge of cold isle and hot isle containment

  • Solid understanding of server hardware and boot process

  • Ability to structure, collaborate and iteratively improve on complex maintenance MOPs.

  • Working with product management, support, and other teams to align operational capabilities with company goals.

  • Translating business priorities into technical and operational requirements.

  • Supporting cross-functional projects where infrastructure plays a critical role.

  • Are action-oriented and willingness to train junior staff on best practices

  • Are willing to travel for bring up of new data center locations as needed

Nice to Have

  • Have 3+ years experience with critical infrastructure systems supporting data centers, such as power distribution, air flow management, environmental monitoring, capacity planning, DCIM software, structured cabling, and cable management

  • Experience with/or knowledge of network topology and configurations and 400gb Infiniband architectures.

  • Experience with/or knowledge of DDP or SCM cluster storage systems.

You will be instrumental in building our leadership team as we scale from startup to market leader.

We're looking for someone who shares our passion for innovation and wants to play a pivotal role in assembling the leadership team that will drive our next phase of growth. This is a great opportunity to help shape the leadership team of a category-defining technology company

  • Partner closely with founders, board members, and existing executives to define role requirements and ideal candidate profiles

  • Leverage extensive candidate networks within the cloud computing and AI industries

  • Proactively build relationships with top-tier executive talent through industry events, conferences, and strategic networking

  • Manage sophisticated interview processes involving multiple stakeholders and assessment methodologies

  • Ensure exceptional candidate experience that reflects our company values and competitive positioning

  • Develop compelling value propositions for executive candidates considering startup opportunities

  • 10+ years of executive search experience, with at least 4 years focused on technology sector roles

  • Proven track record of successfully placing C-suite and VP-level executives at high-growth technology companies

  • Deep understanding of semiconductor, GPU, AI/ML, or related hardware/software technology markets

  • Extensive network of relationships with executive talent in relevant industries

jobs.title: Senior Business Systems Architect
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Jose or San Francisco office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday.


Information Systems at Lambda is responsible for building and scaling the internal systems that power our business. We partner across the company—Finance, GTM, Engineering, and People—to implement tools, automate workflows, and ensure data flows securely and accurately. Our scope includes enterprise applications, integrations, data platform and analytics, compliance automation, and all things IT.


About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Systems Architect to lead and innovate across our enterprise systems landscape. This role will be pivotal in driving operational excellence through the design, implementation, and optimization of scalable business processes and systems integrations. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to create seamless workflows across financial systems, supply chain, and customer-facing platforms while leveraging process automation tools and middleware solutions.

Your responsibilities will include architecting end-to-end solutions, managing complex system integrations, and building a strategic roadmap for enterprise system enhancements. This role requires strong leadership, deep technical expertise, and a proactive approach to solving business challenges.

What You’ll Do

  • Strategic Leadership: Collaborate with stakeholders across functions to develop and execute scalable system roadmaps, aligning business goals with system capabilities.

  • Solution Design & Implementation: Architect and deploy advanced workflows, configurations, and modules in enterprise platforms like NetSuite, Stripe, and Maxio.

  • Tooling Selection & Standardization: Lead the evaluation, selection, and implementation of tools and systems across the business to ensure alignment with operational needs, scalability, and long-term goals. Establish standards for system usage and integration.

  • Integration & Automation: Manage integrations and optimize iPaaS tools (e.g., Workato, Mulesoft) to ensure seamless data flow, interoperability, and automated processes across platforms.

  • Compliance & Optimization: Ensure SOX compliance through robust internal controls, system audits, and governance while driving continuous system improvements.

  • Support & Analytics: Troubleshoot complex issues, mentor team members, and deliver actionable insights through advanced reporting and dashboards to support decision-making.

What We’re Looking For:

  • Experience & Certification:

    • 16+ years of experience in business systems architecture or administration, including hands-on implementation of enterprise systems.

    • Strong understanding of Finance technology stacks, including NetSuite, Billing systems and related business applications

    • Certifications in relevant platforms (e.g., NetSuite Administrator, iPaaS) strongly preferred.

  • Technical Expertise:

    • Proficiency in languages like JavaScript/SQL/ HTML/CSS/Python.

    • Strong experience with middleware and low/no-code platforms (e.g., Workato, Mulesoft, Alteryx) for system integrations & workflow automations.

  • Process Automation:

    • Demonstrated success in deploying process automation tools to streamline operations and increase efficiency.

  • Compliance & Controls:

    • Strong knowledge of ITGCs (IT General Controls) and SOX compliance.

  • Leadership & Communication:

    • Excellent communication skills to effectively collaborate across all levels of leadership—from technical staff to senior executives.

    • Proven ability to present complex technical solutions in a clear and compelling manner

  • AI Automation & Development

    • Hands-on experience leveraging AI and machine learning tools to design, build, and deploy intelligent automation solutions that enhance business processes.

    • Proficiency with AI-powered platforms and frameworks (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, or similar) to develop custom workflows, copilots, and agentic solutions.

    • Proven ability to identify high-impact automation opportunities and translate them into scalable AI-driven implementations across finance and enterprise systems.

Nice to Have

  • Proficiency in system customization tools like SuiteScript, Salesforce Flow, or equivalent.

  • Experience with building Internal AI apps on AWS.

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

About Lambda

  • Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

  • Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

  • We offer generous cash & equity compensation

  • Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

  • Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

  • 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

  • Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

jobs.title: Senior Business Systems Architect - EPM
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco or San Jose office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday

We are seeking an experienced Senior EPM Architect to own and scale our Enterprise Performance Management platform, with Pigment as the core tool. This role will be central to building out Lambda's financial planning, forecasting, and reporting capabilities as the company prepares for and operates as a public company. You will partner closely with FP&A, Accounting, and Business Technology to design and deliver planning models, reporting frameworks, and cross-functional workflows that give leadership real-time visibility into business performance.

Your responsibilities will include architecting Pigment's data model and integration layer, building and maintaining planning and forecasting models, and establishing governance and change management standards for the platform. This role requires deep EPM domain expertise, strong technical chops in Pigment, and the ability to translate finance team needs into scalable, auditable solutions.

What You’ll Do

  • Platform Ownership: Own the end-to-end design, build, and administration of Pigment, including data models, security, user access, and environment management.

  • Planning & Forecasting Models: Architect and maintain revenue, headcount, OpEx, and cash flow planning models in close partnership with FP&A and Accounting leadership.

  • Integration & Data Architecture: Design and manage integrations between Pigment and upstream/downstream systems (NetSuite, BigQuery, Workato, HRIS) to ensure accurate, timely data flow for planning and actuals.

  • Reporting & Analytics: Build executive dashboards, variance analysis, and scenario modeling capabilities that support board-level and investor-grade reporting.

  • SOX & Compliance: Establish and enforce controls around model changes, data access, and audit trails to support SOX and IPO readiness requirements.

  • Stakeholder Enablement: Train and support end users across Finance, drive adoption, and build documentation and governance standards for model changes and release management.

  • Roadmap & Strategy: Develop and maintain a multi-quarter platform roadmap aligned with Lambda's planning maturity goals, partnering with Business Technology and Finance leadership to prioritize enhancements.

You

  • Experience: 8+ years in EPM/FP&A systems, with at least 2+ years of hands-on Pigment implementation and administration experience. Prior experience at a high-growth or pre-IPO/public company strongly preferred.

  • Technical Expertise: Deep proficiency in Pigment's data modeling, formula engine, integration framework, and security model. Working knowledge of SQL, and familiarity with data pipelines (e.g., BigQuery, dbt, Fivetran, Workato).

  • Finance Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of FP&A processes including budgeting, forecasting, revenue planning, headcount modeling, and management reporting. Comfortable speaking the language of Finance and Accounting stakeholders.

  • Compliance & Controls: Practical knowledge of SOX requirements as they apply to financial planning systems, including change management, access controls, and documentation.

  • Communication & Leadership: Ability to work directly with senior Finance leadership and translate ambiguous requirements into structured, scalable solutions. Comfortable presenting to executives and managing competing priorities independently.

  • AI Automation & Development: Hands-on experience leveraging AI/ML tools to enhance financial planning and forecasting workflows, whether through native EPM platform capabilities, custom-built solutions, or third-party AI integrations.

Nice to Have

  • Prior experience with Pigment's API and programmatic integrations.

  • Experience building internal AI-powered apps or prototypes (e.g., on AWS, Vercel, or similar) that augment financial planning or reporting processes.

  • Hands-on familiarity with NetSuite, BigQuery, or Workato.

jobs.descriptionPlain:
Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

If you'd like to build the world's best AI cloud, join us.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Francisco or San Jose office location 4 days per week; Lambda’s designated work from home day is currently Tuesday

We are seeking an experienced Senior EPM Architect to own and scale our Enterprise Performance Management platform, with Pigment as the core tool. This role will be central to building out Lambda's financial planning, forecasting, and reporting capabilities as the company prepares for and operates as a public company. You will partner closely with FP&A, Accounting, and Business Technology to design and deliver planning models, reporting frameworks, and cross-functional workflows that give leadership real-time visibility into business performance.

Your responsibilities will include architecting Pigment's data model and integration layer, building and maintaining planning and forecasting models, and establishing governance and change management standards for the platform. This role requires deep EPM domain expertise, strong technical chops in Pigment, and the ability to translate finance team needs into scalable, auditable solutions.

What You’ll Do

 - Platform Ownership: Own the end-to-end design, build, and administration of Pigment, including data models, security, user access, and environment management.

 - Planning & Forecasting Models: Architect and maintain revenue, headcount, OpEx, and cash flow planning models in close partnership with FP&A and Accounting leadership.

 - Integration & Data Architecture: Design and manage integrations between Pigment and upstream/downstream systems (NetSuite, BigQuery, Workato, HRIS) to ensure accurate, timely data flow for planning and actuals.

 - Reporting & Analytics: Build executive dashboards, variance analysis, and scenario modeling capabilities that support board-level and investor-grade reporting.

 - SOX & Compliance: Establish and enforce controls around model changes, data access, and audit trails to support SOX and IPO readiness requirements.

 - Stakeholder Enablement: Train and support end users across Finance, drive adoption, and build documentation and governance standards for model changes and release management.

 - Roadmap & Strategy: Develop and maintain a multi-quarter platform roadmap aligned with Lambda's planning maturity goals, partnering with Business Technology and Finance leadership to prioritize enhancements.

You

 - Experience: 8+ years in EPM/FP&A systems, with at least 2+ years of hands-on Pigment implementation and administration experience. Prior experience at a high-growth or pre-IPO/public company strongly preferred.

 - Technical Expertise: Deep proficiency in Pigment's data modeling, formula engine, integration framework, and security model. Working knowledge of SQL, and familiarity with data pipelines (e.g., BigQuery, dbt, Fivetran, Workato).

 - Finance Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of FP&A processes including budgeting, forecasting, revenue planning, headcount modeling, and management reporting. Comfortable speaking the language of Finance and Accounting stakeholders.

 - Compliance & Controls: Practical knowledge of SOX requirements as they apply to financial planning systems, including change management, access controls, and documentation.

 - Communication & Leadership: Ability to work directly with senior Finance leadership and translate ambiguous requirements into structured, scalable solutions. Comfortable presenting to executives and managing competing priorities independently.

 - AI Automation & Development: Hands-on experience leveraging AI/ML tools to enhance financial planning and forecasting workflows, whether through native EPM platform capabilities, custom-built solutions, or third-party AI integrations.

Nice to Have

 - Prior experience with Pigment's API and programmatic integrations.

 - Experience building internal AI-powered apps or prototypes (e.g., on AWS, Vercel, or similar) that augment financial planning or reporting processes.

 - Hands-on familiarity with NetSuite, BigQuery, or Workato.

Salary Range Information

The annual salary range for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a salary higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.


About Lambda

 - Founded in 2012, with 500+ employees, and growing fast

 - Our investors notably include TWG Global, US Innovative Technology Fund (USIT), Andra Capital, SGW, Andrej Karpathy, ARK Invest, Fincadia Advisors, G Squared, In-Q-Tel (IQT), KHK & Partners, NVIDIA, Pegatron, Supermicro, Wistron, Wiwynn, Gradient Ventures, Mercato Partners, SVB, 1517, and Crescent Cove

 - We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

 - Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers

 - We offer generous cash & equity compensation

 - Health, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents

 - Wellness and commuter stipends for select roles

 - 401k Plan with 2% company match (USA employees)

 - Flexible paid time off plan that we all actually use

A Final Note:

You do not need to match all of the listed expectations to apply for this position. We are committed to building a team with a variety of backgrounds, experiences, and skills.

Lambda, The Superintelligence Cloud, is a leader in AI cloud infrastructure serving tens of thousands of customers. Our customers range from AI researchers to enterprises and hyperscalers. Lambda's mission is to make compute as ubiquitous as electricity and give everyone the power of superintelligence. One person, one GPU.

*Note: This position requires presence in our San Jose office location 5 days per week.


We are looking for a detail-oriented and dependable Warehouse Associate 3 to join our Warehouse team and support the day-to-day execution of warehouse operations. This role is critical to ensuring timely and accurate material movement, inventory handling, and process adherence across our facility. This job entails becoming a SME in all warehouse processes and being able to take on special projects onsite and other outside warehouse operations. You’ll work closely with the Warehouse Manager and collaborate with cross-functional teams including Accounting, Materials, and Logistics.

What You’ll Do

  • Under limited supervision, applies considerable warehouse knowledge to perform a broad range of tasks which include but are not limited to cycle counting, receiving, put-away, picking/packing, and preparing shipments materials for outbound deployments, and external/internal transfers

  • Handle material movement across designated areas in line with process standards

  • Understand full flow of the materials within warehouse locations.

  • Participate in physical inventories, and reconciliation of activities in coordination with Inventory Control Specialists.

  • Ensure accurate transactions within the ERP system (NetSuite or equivalent)

  • Follow established SOPs and escalate issues when necessary

  • Adhering to all workplace safety policies, reporting safety hazards, and following company regulations.

  • Collaborate with the team to continuously improve warehouse processes and efficiency

  • Support documentation, labeling, and compliance requirements for high-value inventory

  • Ensure the business unit is always audit ready.

  • Assist with training new personnel or cross-train existing personnel on all required warehouse processes.

  • May be required to perform other duties as assigned.

You

  • 3+ years of experience in warehouse or logistics operations preferred.

  • Experience in warehouse, logistics, and materials handling.

  • Familiarity with inventory management systems and ERP software (NetSuite a plus).

  • Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word).

  • Ability to lift up to 50 lbs and operate warehouse equipment (e.g., pallet jacks, forklifts).

  • Excellent organizational and time management skills.

  • Strong communication and problem-solving aptitude.

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.

  • Good communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced. environment

Salary Range Information

This is an hourly role, eligible for overtime. The hourly rate for this position has been set based on market data and other factors. However, a hourly rate higher or lower than this range may be appropriate for a candidate whose qualifications differ meaningfully from those listed in the job description.

  • We have research papers accepted at top machine learning and graphics conferences, including NeurIPS, ICCV, SIGGRAPH, and TOG

  • Our values are publicly available: https://lambda.ai/careers
